fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 70 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(active) == limit` the function incorrectly returns `True` with `remaining=0` instead of `False, 0`.

constraints.intent: When active count equals limit, the strict > condition is False, causing the wrong branch to be taken; this constraint is violated on failing tests.
After computing active, if active count is at or above limit the strict comparison should also be true — this is violated when len(active)==limit.
Before using active in the comparison, if active equals limit the strict > should also hold — this is false when they are equal, exposing the off-by-one bug.
In the else branch (allowed=True), active count must be strictly less than limit; if equal to limit we should not reach here.
